text.title: Experimentation at Scale Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Experiment reports a 'lift' on secondary metric chosen after results were inspected.

root-cause.source: Kohavi et al. — Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments

antipattern.root-cause: No pre-registration; team selected the metric that looked best post-hoc.
antipattern.fix: Enforce pre-registration in templates/experiment-doc.yaml; CI check that doc exists and is signed off before flag is allocated.

antipattern.symptom: Result shows +5% lift but treatment received 47% / control 53% of traffic.
antipattern.root-cause: Traffic splitter bug + no SRM check before reading results.
antipattern.fix: templates/srm_check.py MUST run before any results read; SRM-failing experiments are discarded.

antipattern.symptom: Activation +3%; latency +400ms; ship-decision taken on primary metric alone.
antipattern.fix: Define guardrails per surface (latency, error-rate, revenue per session) and block shipping on degradation > pre-registered threshold.

antipattern.symptom: Team halts experiment at day 4 because 'it looks significant'; replication later shows null.

root-cause.source: Johari et al. — Peeking at A/B Tests (2017)

antipattern.fix: Run to pre-registered sample size; OR pre-register a sequential design (mSPRT, group-sequential).
